name: LANGERHANS CELL HISTIOCYTOSIS
definition: LCH = HISTIOCYTOSIS X (former name) poorly understood group of disorders characterized by abnormal nonmalignant proliferation EOSINOPHILIC GRANULOMA localized often solitary bone lesion as the most benign variety of LCH Hand-Schüller-Christian Disease chronic disseminated form of LCH characterized by CLASSIC triad (in 10-15%) of LETTERER-SIWE DISEASE acute disseminated fulminant form of LCH characterized by wasting, pancytopenia (from

demographics.Path: influx of eosinophilic leukocytes simulating inflammation; reticulum cells accumulate
demographics.Age: any age, mostly presenting at 1-4 years; adults affected in < 30%; < 30 years in 80%;
demographics.Location: bone + bone marrow, lymph nodes, thymus, ear, liver and spleen, gallbladder, GI
demographics.Distribution: bilateral symmetric upper- to mid-lung with sparing of costophrenic angles
demographics.Histo: considerable number of eosinophils in addition to the dominant Langerhans cell

dominant_finding: moderately to markedly enhancing soft-tissue mass with bone erosion
distribution: bone + bone marrow, lymph nodes, thymus, ear, liver and spleen, gallbladder, GI
discriminating_features: characteristic beveled edge = asymmetric destruction of inner + outer cortices
moderately to markedly enhancing soft-tissue mass with bone erosion
low to intermediate SI on T1WI + hyperintense T2WI

XRAY: osteolytic bone lesions 1-15 cm in diameter:
CT: moderately to markedly enhancing soft-tissue mass with bone erosion
MR: low to intermediate SI on T1WI + hyperintense T2WI
NUC: negative bone scans in 35% (radiographs more sensitive)
67Ga may be helpful for detecting nonosseous lesions
Bone: bone lesions: in 80% (most common radiographic manifestation)
characteristic beveled edge = asymmetric destruction of inner + outer cortices
geographic skull = skull lesions may grow in size and coalesce
soft-tissue component : T1-isointense + T2-hyperintense + enhancement; isoechoic
vertebra plana = symmetric uniform vertebral collapse + preservation of intervertebral
lytic expansile aggressive lesion (in early disease)
extramedullary soft-tissue component with decreased T1 + increased T2 signal
increased radiotracer uptake on bone scan; may be falsely negative (not uncommon)
lesion resolution turning sclerotic ← periosteal new bone + sharply defined sclerotic
osteolytic skull lesions with overlying soft-tissue nodules
"geographic skull" = ovoid / serpiginous destruction of large area
destruction of petrous ridge + mastoids + sella turcica
Liver: hypoattenuating, hypoechoic, hypointense T1 and T2 signal along biliary tracts + portal
Lymph Nodes: hard / soft matted groups of nodes → lymphedema
CNS: loss of normal posterior pituitary bright spot
thickening of pituitary stalk (~70%) on CEMR
Lung: usually < 1 cm cysts ± confluence to bulla formation → recurrent spontaneous
pleural effusion + enlarged hilar lymph nodes (rare)
ill-defined diffuse nodular infiltration often progressing to fibrosis + honeycomb lung
Skull (40-50%): beveled edge / "hole-within-hole" appearance ← asymmetric destruction of inner +
sharply marginated without sclerotic rim (DDx: epidermoid with bone sclerosis)
"button sequestrum" = remnants of bone as a central bone density within a lytic lesion
soft-tissue mass overlying the lytic process in calvarium (often palpable)
CNS involvement: thickening of the infundibular stalk > 3 mm
isodense markedly homogeneously enhancing mass in superior aspect of stalk /
absence of posterior pituitary "bright spot" on T1WI
Orbit: osseous destruction + soft-tissue mass extending into orbit / temporal fossa / forehead /
diffuse orbital disease with multiple osteolytic bone lesions
Axial skeleton (8-25%): "vertebra plana" = "coin on edge" = Calvé disease (6%) = collapse of vertebra (most
increased opacity in collapsed vertebral body
Rib (9-15%): ± perilesional edema, especially in early phase
Proximal long bones (15-33%): expansile lytic lesion with ill-defined / sclerotic edges
endosteal scalloping, widening of medullary cavity
laminated periosteal reaction (frequent), may show interruptions
lesions respect joint space + growth plate
Bone involvement (50%):: widespread multiple lytic lesions; "raindrop" pattern in calvarium

clinical.Prognosis: reconstitution of vertebral height is usual
